sections.paragraph:
Σερούχ, ὁ (also Σαρούχ) indecl. (שְׂרוּג; LXX.—Jos., Ant. 1, 148 has Σεροῦγος) Serug, son of Reu and father of Nahor (Gen 11:20–23; 1 Ch 1:26); in the genealogy of Jesus Lk 3:35.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 919
